content.text:
MyOrgAPI — Create User Endpoint

POST /users

The endpoint accepts a JSON payload with the following structure:

{
  "tenant_id": string,
  "user": {
    "email": string,
    "display_name": string,
    "role": string (optional)
  },
  "metadata": {
    "source": "api",
    "version": integer
  }
}

Rules

  1. The email field must be lowercased before submission, except when the tenant_id starts with legacy_. Legacy tenants preserve the original casing for backwards compatibility.
  2. The role field must be omitted from the payload (not set to null, not set to any default value) when the tenant is on the free plan. Free-plan tenants are identified by tenant_id values starting with free_. This rule applies regardless of whether the caller provides a role value — any provided role is discarded for free-plan tenants. For all other tenants, role defaults to "member" if not specified by the caller.
  3. The display_name field must be trimmed of leading and trailing whitespace, with any sequence of internal whitespace collapsed to a single space.
  4. The metadata.version field must be 2 by default, but 1 for any tenant whose ID starts with archive_.

onboarding.taskSummary: Fix MyOrgAPI user payload creation for free-plan tenants by ensuring role is omitted for tenant IDs starting with free_, using docs from the MyOrgAPI create-user spec. Relevant files are user_payload.py and the existing regression tests in test_user_payload.py; a new regression will be added in test_regression.py.

artifactMap.notes: MyOrgAPI create-user endpoint spec and payload rules, including free-plan role omission, email casing, display_name normalization, and metadata versioning.
Current payload builder implementation to update minimally.
Existing unittest coverage for supported behavior; do not modify.

commonCommands.purpose: Run the test suite after adding the regression and after the fix.

constraints: Use guided_tdd_workflow and keep the Centian workflow as the source of truth.
Add the new regression in test_regression.py using standard-library unittest only.
Use project-relative paths and only Centian-exposed project tools.
openQuestions: Whether the free-plan regression should also assert that explicit roles are discarded entirely, not just default roles.

content.text: Task onboarding completed; task moved to planning.

planning.planSummary: Add a new unittest regression for free-plan tenants in test_regression.py that asserts role is omitted for tenant IDs starting with free_. Run the focused test target to confirm it fails, then update user_payload.py minimally so free-plan tenants discard any role and omit the field entirely. Finish by running the regression and existing tests plus a syntax check.
invariants: Keep the fix minimal and limited to payload construction behavior.
Free-plan tenants are identified by tenant_id values starting with free_.

```diff
Index: benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515153904_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_009/project/user_payload.py
===================================================================
--- benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515153904_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_009/project/user_payload.py	original
+++ benchmarks/context_sprawl_v1/results/baseline_no_noise/20260515153904_current_codex_run/runs/current_codex_bug_fix_v1_attempt_009/project/user_payload.py	modified
@@ -7,16 +7,18 @@
         "tenant_id": tenant_id,
         "user": {
             "email": user["email"].lower(),
             "display_name": " ".join(user["display_name"].split()),
-            "role": user.get("role", "member"),
         },
         "metadata": {
             "source": "api",
             "version": 1 if tenant_id.startswith("archive_") else 2,
         },
     }
 
+    if not tenant_id.startswith("free_"):
+        payload["user"]["role"] = user.get("role", "member")
+
     # Legacy tenants preserve email casing for backwards compatibility.
     if tenant_id.startswith("legacy_"):
         payload["user"]["email"] = user["email"]
 
```
